description: OS Native Sandbox - kernel-level file system isolation for bash/MCP tools using macOS Seatbelt (sandbox-exec), preventing LLM from bypassing allowlist via arbitrary scripts

userStories.title: Add sandbox config schema
userStories.description: As a project manager, I need to declare sandbox mode in the config file. Add `sandbox` field to config schema with `enabled: boolean` and `paths?: string[]`.
acceptanceCriteria: In `packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts`, add `sandbox` field to config schema with Zod
`sandbox.enabled` is boolean, defaults to false
`sandbox.paths` is optional string array for extra allowed paths
No `sandbox` field in config = behavior unchanged (backward compatible)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define Sandbox interface and getSandbox factory
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a cross-platform Sandbox interface so tool code doesn't need to know the platform-specific implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/opencode/src/sandbox/index.ts` with `Sandbox` interface
Interface has: `wrap(command: string[]): string[]`, `isAvailable(): Promise<boolean>`, `generatePolicy(config): Promise<string>`
`getSandbox()` factory returns platform-specific implementation or `null` if unsupported
On macOS (`process.platform === 'darwin'`), returns `SeatbeltSandbox` instance (stubbed, implemented in next story)
On unsupported platforms, returns `null`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement SBPL profile generation from allowlist/deny config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to convert allowlist/deny rules from .opencode-security.json into a Seatbelt SBPL profile string.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/opencode/src/sandbox/profile.ts` with `generateProfile()` function
Directory glob patterns (e.g. `src/**`) â†’ extract top-level dir â†’ `(allow file-read* file-write* (subpath "/abs/path/src"))`
File patterns (e.g. `package.json`) â†’ `(allow file-read* file-write* (literal "/abs/path/package.json"))`
Deny rule directories â†’ `(deny file-read* file-write* (subpath ...))` written AFTER allow rules (last match wins)
Deny rule files â†’ `(deny file-read* file-write* (literal ...))`
All allowlist entries are rw (no read-only distinction)
All paths resolved via `fs.realpath()` before writing to profile (handles symlinks like /tmp â†’ /private/tmp)
Profile starts with `(version 1)` and `(deny default)`
Unit tests cover: directory glob, file pattern, deny directory, deny file, empty allowlist, symlink resolution
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add built-in whitelist and process capabilities to SBPL profile
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the generated SBPL profile to include system paths, dev tool paths, and basic process capabilities so sandboxed commands can actually run.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile includes process capabilities: `process-exec`, `process-fork`, `signal`, `pseudo-tty`, `sysctl-read`, required `mach-lookup` services
Profile allows read-only access to system binaries: `/usr/bin`, `/bin`, `/usr/sbin`, `/sbin`
Profile allows read-only access to system libraries: `/usr/lib`, `/usr/share`, `/System/Library`, `/Library/Frameworks`
Profile allows read-only access to Homebrew: `/opt/homebrew`, `/usr/local`
Profile allows read-only access to Xcode: `/Applications/Xcode.app`, `/Library/Developer`
Profile allows read-only access to /dev devices: `/dev/null`, `/dev/zero`, `/dev/ptmx`, `/dev/ttysN`
Profile allows rw access to project `node_modules/` and `.git/` directories
Profile allows rw access to `/private/tmp`
Profile allows all network: `(allow network*)`
Auto-detect and allow read-only access to user toolchain paths: `~/.bun`, `~/.nvm`, `~/.cargo`, `~/.pyenv` (only if they exist)
User-configured `sandbox.paths` added as rw allow rules
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement SeatbeltSandbox class
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the macOS Seatbelt implementation of the Sandbox interface that generates profile files and wraps commands with sandbox-exec.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/opencode/src/sandbox/seatbelt.ts` with `SeatbeltSandbox` class implementing `Sandbox` interface
`isAvailable()` checks `sandbox-exec` exists via `which sandbox-exec`
`generatePolicy(config)` calls profile generation from US-003/US-004, writes to temp file, returns path
`wrap(command)` prepends `['sandbox-exec', '-f', policyPath]` to the command array
Generated profile file is cached per session (not regenerated on every call)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Adapt bash tool for sandbox execution
userStories.description: As a user, I want bash commands to automatically run inside the sandbox when sandbox is enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify `packages/opencode/src/tool/bash.ts` to check if sandbox is enabled
When sandbox enabled, wrap bash command with `sandbox.wrap()` before spawning
stdout/stderr correctly passed through
Exit code correctly passed through
Timeout mechanism still works
Working directory is project root
Skip bash scanner security checks when sandbox is enabled (sandbox provides stronger OS-level isolation)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Adapt interactive bash tool for sandbox execution
userStories.description: As a user, I want interactive bash sessions to also run inside the sandbox.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify `packages/opencode/src/tool/interactive-bash.ts` to check if sandbox is enabled
When sandbox enabled, wrap interactive bash with `sandbox.wrap()` before spawning
PTY/interactive session works correctly under sandbox-exec
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Adapt MCP tool for sandbox execution
userStories.description: As a user, I want MCP servers to run inside the sandbox to prevent bypassing file restrictions through MCP tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify MCP server launch in `packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts` to wrap with `sandbox.wrap()` when sandbox enabled
MCP server stdio pipes work correctly under sandbox-exec
MCP server file access is restricted by sandbox profile
`enforced` policy MCP servers always run in sandbox
`trusted` policy MCP servers skip sandbox (user's explicit trust decision)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify file tools use application-layer allowlist (no sandbox)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm file tools (read/write/glob/grep) continue using application-layer allowlist checks and do NOT go through sandbox-exec.
acceptanceCriteria: read/write/glob/grep tools in sandbox mode continue using existing allowlist checks
No sandbox-exec wrapping for file tools â€” OpenCode controls this code, LLM cannot bypass
Verify existing allowlist check logic covers all file tool paths
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement sandbox initialization flow
userStories.description: As a user, when sandbox is enabled, I want automatic initialization at session start with clear progress and error messages.
acceptanceCriteria: On session start, detect `sandbox.enabled = true` and trigger init flow
Init steps: 1) platform detection (sandbox-exec available), 2) environment check (macOS version, SIP), 3) policy generation, 4) validation (run `sandbox-exec -f profile.sb /usr/bin/true`)
If any step fails, sandbox auto-disables, session continues without sandbox, warning shown
Init result cached at session level â€” no re-init unless allowlist config changes
Clear error messages for each failure type: platform unsupported, profile generation failed, validation failed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement sandbox doctor CLI command
userStories.description: As a user, I need `opencode sandbox doctor` to diagnose my sandbox environment without starting the TUI.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/opencode/src/sandbox/doctor.ts` with environment detection logic
macOS checks: sandbox-exec available, macOS version, SIP status
Each failed check includes specific fix instructions
New CLI command `opencode sandbox doctor` registered in CLI commands
Command runs checks and prints results to stdout (no TUI needed)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add sandbox status to TUI and CLI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see sandbox status in the TUI status bar and via `opencode sandbox status` command.
acceptanceCriteria: TUI status bar shows sandbox state: `[Sandbox: ON]`, `[Sandbox: OFF]`, or `[Sandbox: FAILED]`
Sandbox errors shown in TUI with repair suggestions
`opencode sandbox status` CLI command shows: platform, sandbox type, init status, allowlist paths, built-in whitelist paths
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integration tests for sandbox security
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests verifying sandbox correctly blocks access to protected files and allows access to permitted files.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests written against Sandbox interface (shared between macOS and future Linux)
Test: sandboxed bash cannot read files outside allowlist (Operation not permitted)
Test: sandboxed `python3 -c "open('secrets/key.pem').read()"` fails with permission denied
Test: sandboxed bash CAN read/write files in allowlist
Test: deny rule paths blocked even when parent is in allowlist
Test: built-in whitelist paths (node_modules, .git, /tmp) accessible
Test: user-configured extra paths accessible
Test: unsupported platform gracefully degrades (skips sandbox)
macOS-specific unit tests for SBPL profile generation correctness
Skip sandbox tests on unsupported platforms via `process.platform` check
All tests pass via `bun test --cwd packages/opencode`
Typecheck passes
